author: mderazon
description: Organize notes into Kanban boards using frontmatter properties. Drag and drop cards between columns powered by Bases. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.description: Organize notes into Kanban boards using frontmatter properties. Drag and drop cards between columns powered by Bases.

name: Base Board

releases.author: mderazon
releases.description:
### What's Changed
* **Self-healing folder rename sync** by @B-ki — automatically updates `.base` board filters when folders are renamed or moved.
* **Work in Progress (WIP) limits** by @nvkv — configure maximum card limits per column with visual overflow highlighting.
* **Clean cover card thumbnails** by @mderazon — high-performance, synchronous metadata-driven cover images (via `cover` property).
* **Fix column and card renaming** by @Konstantin-Lasiy — resolves the inline input `HierarchyRequestError` on recent Obsidian releases.

releases.author: mderazon
releases.description:
## What's New in v2.2.0

This release focuses on aligning card navigation with native Obsidian patterns, introducing column coloring, and enhancing database view integrations.

### Column Coloring & Customizer
- **Accent Lines & Background Tint:** You can now color-code columns by right-clicking their headers. Colored columns display a top accent line and a subtle background color tint matching the selection.
- **Color Reset:** Added a "Reset to default" action in the color picker modal to clear column/tag colors back to their defaults.

### Obsidian-Native Card Navigation & Multi-Select
- **Active Pane Open (New Default):** Clicking a card opens the note directly in your active editor pane/tab by default, reusing it when changing notes to avoid cluttering your workspace.
- **Native Keyboard Modifiers:** Fully respects standard Obsidian keyboard shortcuts on click:
  - `Ctrl/Cmd + Click` opens the card note in a new tab.
  - `Ctrl/Cmd + Alt + Click` (or `Cmd + Option + Click` on macOS) opens the note in a split pane to the side.
- **Alt/Option + Click Multi-Select:** Shifted the multi-selection toggle key to `Alt/Option + Click` (and `Shift + Click` for range selection), matching Obsidian's File Explorer convention and resolving navigation shortcut conflicts.
- **Configure open behavior:** Keep `"modal"`, `"split"`, or `"tab"` if you prefer custom plain-click actions.

### Bases Integration & General Fixes
- **createFileForView Routing:** Card creation now correctly routes through Bases' folder/template configuration rather than dumping files directly in root.
- **minAppVersion Bump:** Bumped to `1.10.2` to support the latest core Bases capabilities.
- **Folder Rename Fix:** Fixed a layout-breaking bug on folder renaming by aligning the internal empty-value column mapping (`""` <=> `NO_VALUE_COLUMN`).
- **No Value Deletion:** Added right-click deletion support for the "No Value" column when it contains no cards.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v2.1.3

- Formula properties are no longer skipped during card chip rendering — formula-derived values now show as chips alongside frontmatter properties (#19)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v2.1.2

- Style the "+N more" / "show less" toggle button to match chip box model (padding, border-radius, flex layout)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v2.1.1

- Replace `display: contents` with `display: flex` for overflow chip container to fix partial browser support warning (#14 follow-up)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in v2.1.0

- **Column scroll preservation** — per-column vertical scroll position is now preserved when cards are deleted or moved between columns (#17)
- **Card chip overflow** — first 4 chips are visible on each card with a "+N more" toggle to expand the remaining chips (#14)
- **Configurable card title** — new `cardTitleProperty` view option in `.base` files lets you use a frontmatter property (e.g. `title`) as the card heading instead of the filename (#16)
- **`data-property-id` on chips** — chip elements now carry a `data-property-id` attribute for CSS theming, so snippets can target specific properties reliably (#15)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Breaking changes

- **CLI and MCP packages removed** from this repository. This was required to publish the plugin as a community plugin in Obsidian.

## What's new

- **Wikilinks rendered as display text** in card property chips — `[[Mario]]` now shows as `Mario`, `[[Notes|Alias]]` shows as `Alias` (@mariobennekersvallejo, #10)

## Bug fixes

- **Scroll position preserved** across board re-renders — no more jumping back to column 0 when metadata updates
- **`#` prefix stripped from tags** — tags no longer intermittently show as `#feature` instead of `feature` (#6)
- **Empty boards show columns** — configured columns remain visible even when all cards are deleted, so you can still add new ones (#9)
- **Drag-drop console errors fixed** — eliminated `HierarchyRequestError` spam when dragging cards
- **ESLint peer dependency conflict** resolved — `npm ci` now works without flags (#3)
- **minAppVersion bumped to 1.10.0** — required for Bases support

## Contributors

- @mariobennekersvallejo — wikilink rendering (#10)

releases.author: mderazon
releases.description:
## ✨ What's new in 1.4.0

### Column Header Improvements

- **Delete column in context menu** — Right-click the column header to access both Rename and Delete. The delete item shows the number of cards that will remain (e.g. *"Delete column (3 cards will remain)"*) and is styled as a destructive action.
- **Rename via context menu only** — Double-click to rename has been removed. All column actions are now consolidated in the right-click context menu for a cleaner, more intentional interaction.
- **Cleaner rename experience** — When renaming a column, the card count badge and the `+` button are hidden so the input can use the full header width. They reappear immediately when you confirm or cancel.
- **Count badge closer to title** — The card count is now tighter next to the column name, reducing visual clutter.
- **Add card moved to column header**

### Multi-select Drag-and-Drop

- **Drag a group** — When you have multiple cards selected (Cmd/Ctrl+click or Shift+click) and drag any one of them, the entire selection moves together.
- **Stacked ghost** — The drag image shows a stack of cards with an accent-colored badge indicating how many cards are being dragged (e.g. `3`).
- **Ghost dimming** — All other selected cards dim on the board while the drag is in progress, making it clear which cards are part of the move.
- **Precise drop placement** — The card you grab lands exactly where you drop it. The remaining selected cards are inserted immediately after it, preserving their relative order.
- **Cancel restores everything** — Dropping outside a valid column cancels the drag and snaps all cards back to their original positions.

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed cards disappearing when dropped outside a column drop zone.
- Fixed the column list being clipped at the bottom when a filter bar is active above the board.
releases.name: 1.4.0 — Column header UX & multi-select drag-and-drop

- Replaced static view with Native Obsidian Editor inside Card Detail Modal.
- Added a new tokenizing Tag Editor.
- Polished CSS and aligned action buttons.
- Removed duplicate modal header.
- Updated Card action menu 'Open' to trigger the new Modal.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.2.2

### ✨ Idiomatic Tag Naming
- Renamed all internal and user-facing "Labels" to **"Tags"** to be more consistent with the Obsidian engine and its first-class property support. This includes renaming the "Edit labels" menu option and the tag colors setting.

### 📋 Improved Onboarding Experience
- **Smarter Scaffolding**: The "Create new board" command now defaults to a 3-column setup (**To Do**, **In Progress**, **Done**).
- **Richer Sample Notes**: Added 5 sample task notes with pre-populated tags (`planning`, `research`, `feature`, `bug`, `docs`) so users immediately see their board in action with vibrant colors.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Fixed in v1.2.1

### 🛠️ Column Drag & Drop Improvements
- **Smoother Column Reordering**: Fixed the "sticky" feeling when dragging columns. Columns now respond instantly and move fluidly to the target position.
- **Improved Grabbing**: You can now drag a column by grabbing anywhere in the **column header**, not just the small icon.
- **Zero Layout Shift**: Fixed a layout jump that occurred when a column was picked up. The placeholder now matches the column width exactly.
- **Clean Visuals**: The browser's drag ghost now shows only the column header instead of the entire tall column for a much cleaner look.

### 📋 Background Stability
- Improved drag event cleanup to ensure no stale ghosts or placeholders remain after a drop.
- Added guards to prevent accidental drags when double-clicking to rename columns.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.2.0

### ✨ Hover Preview
- **Native Card Preview**: Hover over any card to see a preview of its content using Obsidian's built-in **Page preview** core plugin. (Hold `Ctrl`/`Cmd` while hovering if the modifier key is enabled in your settings).

### 🏷️ Labels & Filter Bar
- **Improved Color Picker**: Replaced the previous color picker with a native Obsidian `Modal` for better accessibility, including behavior for the `Esc` key and focus management.
- **Discoverability**: Added tooltips to the label filter pills to inform users that they can **Click to filter** and **Right-click to change color**.
- **Performance**: Optimized the rendering of cards by internalizing the property skip list.

### 📋 Property Chips
- **Better Date/Time Formatting**: Property chips for dates now display relative times (e.g., '3 days ago') using the official Bases API.
- **Improved Property Logic**: Added support for more `file.*` properties like `file.ctime` and `file.mtime` while maintaining clean card layouts by skipping redundant properties.

### 🛠️ Bug Fixes & Refinement
- Fixed an issue where property chips weren't showing due to incorrect internal API usage.
- Standardized property icons and display names to respect user configuration.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

### 🏷️ Labels — color-coded tags on cards

Cards now display their frontmatter `tags` as colored label chips at the top. Each tag gets a consistent color automatically, and you can customize any color by **right-clicking** a label.

A **filter bar** appears at the top of the board showing all labels in use — click any label to filter the board to only cards with that tag.

You can also edit a card's labels directly from the board via the card edit menu (pencil icon on hover).

### 🐛 Bug Fix: Property chips now actually show up on cards

The "Rich Cards" feature (showing metadata fields as chips on each card) was silently broken — the code was reading the list of enabled properties through incorrect internal paths instead of the official Bases API, so no chips were ever rendered regardless of what you had toggled on in the Properties menu.

**Fixed:** the plugin now correctly reads the user-configured property list via `config.getOrder()`.

### ✨ File properties (created time, modified time, size) now supported

Previously, all `file.*` properties were blocked from appearing as chips. Only the ones that don't make sense on a card are now skipped (name, path, links, embeds, tags). Useful properties now show up:

- **Created time** → e.g. `created time · 3 days ago`
- **Modified time** → e.g. `modified time · 2 hours ago`
- **File size**, **Folder** → also supported

### ✨ Date/time chips display as relative time

Date and time values now render as human-friendly relative strings (e.g. *"3 days ago"*, *"in 2 hours"*) instead of raw ISO timestamps.

### ✨ Property display names respected

Chip labels now use `config.getDisplayName()`, which respects any custom property renames you've set in your `.base` file.
